title: M4A Audio
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents an M4A file, an audio-only MPEG-4 container typically carrying AAC-encoded audio. M4A is the default format for purchased or downloaded audio on Apple platforms and is widely used in iTunes and Apple Music libraries.

## When to use
Use `media:m4a;audio` when a node produces or consumes M4A files, such as Apple Music exports or iOS audio recordings. For the raw AAC stream without the MP4 container, see [AAC Audio](https://capdag.com/browse/media:aac;audio). For lossless audio, see [FLAC Audio](https://capdag.com/browse/media:flac;audio) or [AIFF Audio](https://capdag.com/browse/media:aiff;audio).

## Notes
M4A files use the `.m4a` extension but are technically MP4 containers. Consumers that handle MP4 video can generally demux M4A files as well. DRM-protected M4A files (M4P) cannot be decoded without the appropriate licence.
